background.occupation: IT Project Manager at a large insurance corporation
background.social_class: Middle Class (homeowner in suburban New Jersey, two kids in public schools)
background.key_experience: In 2018, his company's rushed implementation of a 'smart office' system led to constant surveillance of bathroom breaks and keyboard activity, which he had to enforce as a manager - making him deeply distrustful of workplace tech that prioritizes efficiency over human dignity.

dominant_traits: Extremely High Agreeableness (to the point of conflict avoidance)
Moderately Low Openness to Experience (skeptical of tech hype)

cognition.core_value: Workplace autonomy - believes adults should be trusted to manage their own time without digital micromanagement
cognition.bias: Anchoring bias - once he sees a tech implementation fail (like the 2018 system), he assumes ALL similar technologies will have the same flaws, triggered by any mention of 'productivity tracking' or 'workplace optimization'

current_state.recent_memory: Last Thursday, his CEO announced mandatory installation of employee monitoring software that tracks active vs. idle time on work computers
current_state.emotion: Smoldering resentment - outwardly polite but internally furious about yet another dehumanizing tech mandate from leadership
